codeReview.summary: Worker correctly determined no schema changes were needed. The existing `kode TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL` field in coaDatabase.ts already supports GL Account codes from Excel (e.g., '11-11-001') because TEXT in SQLite stores any string including dashes. COAListPage.tsx uses `font-mono` class for kode display which properly renders formatted codes. No code changes required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No convention violations found. Worker correctly followed existing patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker verified schema compatibility through typecheck and lint. No code changes were necessary.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed backend-worker skill procedure correctly. No deviations noted in skillFeedback.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker read mission.md, validation-contract.md, located coaDatabase.ts, ran typecheck, ran lint, and prepared handoff. skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker used only npm run typecheck and npm run lint commands - both are documented in services.yaml.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: No undocumented services or commands were used.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new knowledge gaps discovered. The finding that TEXT field supports any string format including dashes ('11-11-001') is straightforward SQLite behavior already documented in architecture.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md defines `kode TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL` for COA table. SQLite TEXT type supports arbitrary strings.

summary: Review PASS. Worker correctly identified that the COA database schema already supports GL Account codes from Excel (e.g., '11-11-001') without any changes. The existing `kode TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL` field stores any string format including dashes. COAListPage uses `font-mono` for kode display. Both typecheck and lint passed with 0 errors. No blocking or non-blocking issues found.
